registers.name: g_mode
registers.comment: 0xA4 G_MODE — interrupt mode. 0 = polling, 1 = trigger (INT pulses on a touch event). Written at init from int_gpio presence.

registers.name: th_group
registers.comment: 0x80 TH_GROUP — touch-detection threshold. Written at init from touch_threshold.

registers.name: periodactive
registers.comment: 0x88 PERIODACTIVE — active-mode scan/report period. Written at init from active_period.

registers.name: td_status
registers.comment: 0x02 TD_STATUS — bits[3:0] = number of active touch points (0..2).

registers.name: touch1
registers.comment: 0x03..0x06 first touch point: [XH, XL, YH, YL]. XH bits[3:0]=X[11:8] (bits[7:6]=event flag); XL=X[7:0]; YH bits[3:0]=Y[11:8] (bits[7:4]=touch id); YL=Y[7:0]. 12-bit, big-endian.

init_sequence.comment: INT pin configured -> trigger (interrupt) mode; no INT pin -> polling mode.
Apply the user active-mode report period.

fields.label: pressed
fields.comment: TD_STATUS low nibble = touch count; 0 = released, 1..2 = pressed. Engine bool conversion is value != 0.

fields.label: x
fields.comment: 0x03..0x04 assembled big-endian, masked to 12 bits (drops the event-flag nibble). Valid only when pressed; sticky between touches.

fields.label: y
fields.comment: 0x05..0x06 assembled big-endian, masked to 12 bits (drops the touch-id nibble). Valid only when pressed; sticky between touches.
